objectClass.notes: This is a repository-authored procedural benchmark, not an image reconstruction. Constraint limits are authoring inputs and the preview timeline is not solver authority.

reasoning: A serial base-yaw, shoulder, elbow, and shade hierarchy must preserve local axes, limits, sockets, and exact authored rest transforms.
Paired structural rails, tension springs, cable paths, hinge hardware, and a bell reflector create an interrupted product silhouette.
Three visual tiers must reduce radial, spring, and fastener geometry while preserving every protected semantic and collider-construction-input ID.

specDepthDecision.rationale: Serial articulation, visible attachments, multiple material zones, and mobile tessellation require an explicit action-ready hierarchy.

definitionOfDone: The design and profile cameras read as a weighted-base articulated task lamp with paired lower/upper arms, exposed hardware, paired exterior/interior annular crown faces and rolled overlap, an intentionally open lower reflector aperture, and a warm stylized opaque bulb.
Action-ready mode visibly exercises all four authored rotation channels and exact reset restores seed-bounded rest transforms.

Every tier preserves protected components, 19 attachment/joint sockets, four ConstraintConstructionInput records, 15 ColliderConstructionInput records, destruction groups, and one-render/no-post ownership.

featureGroups.name: Weighted base and yaw post
qualityCriteria: The low circular base establishes stable support and the offset yaw post reads as the serial-chain root.

featureGroups.name: Yaw, shoulder, elbow, and shade hinges
qualityCriteria: All pivots use named local axes, readable joint hardware, authored limits, and stable parent-child ownership.

featureGroups.name: Paired arms, springs, and cables
qualityCriteria: Two rails per stage preserve spacing while every decorative parent-local spring and cable sweep publishes named start/end sockets, actual sweep radii, overlap, and gap tolerances; spring collars visibly penetrate the receiving washer region without claiming cross-joint mechanics or load transfer.
failureModes: metadata-only contact that disagrees with the rendered spring radius

featureGroups.name: Bell reflector and warm bulb
qualityCriteria: The shade retains paired exterior-enamel and underside-reflector crown annuli with rolled shell overlap, a deliberately open lower aperture, inner reflector, rim, socket, stylized opaque bulb, and emission socket.
failureModes: front-sided crown disappearing from the underside view

featureGroups.name: Action-ready preview and physics boundary
qualityCriteria: Four deterministic absolute-time channels reset exactly; all four constraints and 15 colliders remain complete construction inputs with solver authority false. One exact capped cylinder owns the solid shade neck while eight separate tier-invariant capsules approximate only the hollow shade boundary and preserve the open lower aperture.
failureModes: shade ribs incorrectly absorbing the neck collision role

antiShallowSpecRules: Reject a single bent tube in place of the serial hierarchy.
Reject hinges without named pivots and sockets.
Reject floating cables, springs, or paired arm rails.
Reject a shade proxy that fills the authored open aperture or lacks a declared bidirectional surface-deviation bound.
Reject a missing shade-neck cylinder or a neck proxy whose caps, frame, or 0.0017 metre bidirectional bound do not match the actual indexed neck mesh.
Reject solver or visual-acceptance claims without adapters and evidence.

authoringInstruction: Use this assessment as an authored benchmark contract. It does not constitute reference-image, solver, or visual-acceptance evidence.
